steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find angle L in radians
steps.content:
Angle K measures $\frac{\pi}{32}$ radians. Since angle L is 16 times angle K:
$$L = 16K = 16 \cdot \frac{\pi}{32} = \frac{16\pi}{32} = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Convert π/2 radians to degrees
steps.content:
Use the conversion factor: $\text{degrees} = \text{radians} \times \frac{180}{\pi}$
$$L = \frac{\pi}{2} \times \frac{180}{\pi} = \frac{180}{2} = 90^{\circ}$$
That confirms B is correct.
